message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Step 1 done, starting Step 2: Inbox Capture">
Task #11 (Navigation + Dashboard) is committed and complete (f71047e). Moving to Task #15: Implementation Step 2 — Inbox Capture (US-GTD-1). Luca remains Driver. Please review his work as it progresses and send feedback from your area of expertise.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Good -- Task #11 is committed. Let me orient on Task #15 (Inbox Capture) and prepare to review Luca's work against US-GTD-1 acceptance criteria.

content.content:
#1 [completed] Product Discovery: Research GTD and define product scope (marty-cagan)
#11 [completed] Implementation Step 1: Navigation + Dashboard skeleton (luca-palmieri)
#15 [in_progress] Implementation Step 2: Inbox Capture (US-GTD-1) (luca-palmieri)

tasks.subject: Product Discovery: Research GTD and define product scope

content.content:
Task #15: Implementation Step 2: Inbox Capture (US-GTD-1)
Status: in_progress
Description: Build the Inbox capture functionality — the entry point for the GTD system.

Deliverables per US-GTD-1:
1. Database: Create inbox_items table (id UUID, user_id UUID FK, title TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ)
2. Domain: InboxItem type with validated Title
3. Routes: GET /inbox (list inbox items), POST /inbox (capture new item)
4. Template: inbox.html with item list ordered oldest-first (FIFO for processing)
5. Quick capture form wired up (header form already exists from Step 1)
6. Inbox count badge shows real count from database
7. HTMX enhancement: capture without full page reload
8. Delete inbox items
9. Works without JavaScript (progressive enhancement)
10. Integration tests + e2e test updates

Key constraints:
- Inbox items show oldest-first (for processing order)
- No categorization at capture time
- Capture must be fast (under 3 seconds)
- Inbox count visible in nav badge
- Quick capture available on every authenticated page via header input

Follow TDD, Atomic Green Step pipeline. Luca drives.

file.content:
## Part 2: V1 GTD (Getting Things Done)

The product is evolving from a simple todo list into a full GTD system. These stories
define the V1 GTD scope. See `docs/gtd-product-discovery.md` for the complete product
discovery document.

### US-GTD-1: Capture to Inbox

**As a** GTD practitioner,
**I want to** quickly capture anything on my mind into my inbox,
**So that** I can get it out of my head and process it later.

#### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Inbox capture input is always accessible (prominent on the main page, and available
  via a persistent capture input on every authenticated page)
- [ ] Capture is fast: type and press Enter, item appears in inbox
- [ ] No categorization required at capture time (that happens during clarify)
- [ ] Inbox items show in a list ordered by capture time (oldest first -- process in
  FIFO order, like a physical inbox)
- [ ] Inbox count is visible in the navigation so users know how many items need processing
- [ ] Input has a visible `<label>` and helpful placeholder text
- [ ] After capturing, the input field is cleared and focus returns to it
- [ ] Empty or whitespace-only submissions are silently ignored
- [ ] Title has a maximum length of 300 characters
- [ ] Works without JavaScript (full page reload); HTMX enhances
- [ ] `aria-live` region announces "Captured to inbox" after HTMX swap
- [ ] Capture event is logged with structured tracing

#### Accessibility
- [ ] All interactive elements are fully keyboard operable
- [ ] Capture input has a visible `<label>` associated via `for`/`id`
- [ ] Focus management: after capture, focus returns to the input
- [ ] Skip link bypasses header capture input to reach main content
- [ ] Screen reader announces inbox count change after capture

#### Empty State
- When the inbox is empty, show: "Inbox zero -- nothing to process. Capture something
  new or start your weekly review." with links to capture input and weekly review.

#### Edge Cases
- Adding with only whitespace (silently ignored)
- Rapid successive captures
- Very long text (at or beyond max length)
- Special characters, HTML, emoji

#### Success Metric
Capturing a thought takes under 3 seconds from thought to inbox.

---

content.text: Good. I now have the full US-GTD-1 acceptance criteria. Let me check the current state of the working tree to see if Luca has started.

input.description: Check code file changes for Task #15

content.text: Clean working tree. Luca hasn't started coding yet. Let me send him a product brief with the key acceptance criteria to watch for.

input.content:
Luca, congratulations on Task #11 -- clean commit. Here's my product brief for Task #15 (Inbox Capture, US-GTD-1):

**Critical product requirements:**

1. **Domain constraint (from Scott):** Capture ALWAYS creates an `InboxItem`, never a `NextAction` directly. Even from the header quick capture. This preserves the GTD "capture now, decide later" principle.

2. **Inbox ordering:** Oldest first (FIFO). This is how a physical inbox works -- process the item that's been sitting longest. New items go to the bottom.

3. **No categorization at capture:** The inbox capture form takes ONLY a title. No context, no project, no category. Categorization happens during the Clarify step (step 5).

4. **Inbox count in nav:** The nav should show real counts: "Inbox (3)". When count is zero, just "Inbox" with no number. This was agreed by Steve Schoger -- calm, not alarming. This means every authenticated page needs to know the user's inbox count to render the nav.

5. **Quick capture from header (already built):** The form in `gtd_nav.html` already POSTs to `/inbox`. Now you need the POST handler to actually create an `InboxItem`. After capture: clear input, focus returns to input, aria-live announces "Captured to inbox."

6. **Empty state:** When inbox is empty, show: "Inbox zero -- nothing to process. Capture something new or start your weekly review." with links to the capture input focus and `/review`.

7. **Silent ignore for empty/whitespace:** Don't show an error for empty submissions. Just silently ignore them -- an accidental Enter press is not worth an error message.

8. **Max length 300 chars:** Same validation as the existing `TodoTitle`. Consider renaming to `ItemTitle` (per glossary) or reusing the existing type.

9. **Delete inbox items:** Users should be able to delete items from inbox without going through clarify (e.g., captured something by mistake).

10. **Duplicate capture input on inbox page:** US-GTD-8 says "on the Inbox page, this header input and the inbox page's own capture input are the same form posting to the same endpoint -- avoid showing two capture inputs on the inbox page." The header capture IS the inbox page's capture form. Don't add a second one in the page body.

**Schema (per Luca + Scott agreement):** Create `inbox_items` table as a separate table (not in the unified `actions` table -- inbox items have a distinct lifecycle).

Looking forward to reviewing your TDD implementation.
input.summary: Product brief for Task #15: Inbox Capture requirements

**Critical product requirements:**

3. **No categorization at capture:** The inbox capture form takes ONLY a title. No context, no project, no category. Categorization happens during the Clarify step (step 5).

4. **Inbox count in nav:** The nav should show real counts: "Inbox (3)". When count is zero, just "Inbox" with no number. This was agreed by Steve Schoger -- calm, not alarming. This means every authenticated page needs to know the user's inbox count to render the nav.

5. **Quick capture from header (already built):** The form in `gtd_nav.html` already POSTs to `/inbox`. Now you need the POST handler to actually create an `InboxItem`. After capture: clear input, focus returns to input, aria-live announces "Captured to inbox."

6. **Empty state:** When inbox is empty, show: "Inbox zero -- nothing to process. Capture something new or start your weekly review." with links to the capture input focus and `/review`.

7. **Silent ignore for empty/whitespace:** Don't show an error for empty submissions. Just silently ignore them -- an accidental Enter press is not worth an error message.

8. **Max length 300 chars:** Same validation as the existing `TodoTitle`. Consider renaming to `ItemTitle` (per glossary) or reusing the existing type.

9. **Delete inbox items:** Users should be able to delete items from inbox without going through clarify (e.g., captured something by mistake).

10. **Duplicate capture input on inbox page:** US-GTD-8 says "on the Inbox page, this header input and the inbox page's own capture input are the same form posting to the same endpoint -- avoid showing two capture inputs on the inbox page." The header capture IS the inbox page's capture form. Don't add a second one in the page body.

**Schema (per Luca + Scott agreement):** Create `inbox_items` table as a separate table (not in the unified `actions` table -- inbox items have a distinct lifecycle).

Looking forward to reviewing your TDD implementation."
  }
}

content.text: Task #11 is complete. I've sent my product brief for Task #15 (Inbox Capture) to Luca with all 10 key requirements from US-GTD-1. Clean working tree -- Luca hasn't started coding yet. Standing by to review his TDD implementation as it progresses.
